architecture.summary: SynCode is a constrained decoding framework that augments LLM token generation with grammar-based filtering. During each decoding step, the system parses partial code to extract a terminal sequence and remainder, uses an offline-precomputed DFA mask store to compute which tokens are syntactically valid continuations, masks the LLM's logit distribution to zero out invalid tokens, and samples the next token from the constrained distribution. The DFA mask store is built once per grammar-vocabulary pair by precomputing binary masks for DFA states and terminal sequences, enabling efficient O(|remainder| + |accept_sequences|) per-token masking during generation.

biblio.title: SynCode: LLM Generation with Grammar Augmentation

claims.statement: SynCode reduces syntax errors by 96.07% when combined with state-of-the-art LLMs for Python and Go code generation
For Python on HumanEval, Llama-7B with SynCode achieves 99% syntax error reduction (230 errors → 2 errors)
For Python on MBXP, CodeGen-350M with SynCode achieves 100% syntax error reduction (68 errors → 0 errors)
SynCode introduces a 1.22x average runtime overhead with LR(1) parsing and 1.76x with LALR(1) parsing across models and languages
For Go on HumanEval, Llama-7B with SynCode reduces syntax errors by 99% (725 → 10)
SynCode achieves soundness: all syntactically valid tokens are retained in the mask for accept sequences of length ≥ 1
SynCode achieves completeness: all syntactically invalid tokens are rejected when accept sequences have length greater than the maximum token string length in the vocabulary
Remaining syntax errors (3.93% of total) occur because the LLM fails to halt generation before reaching the maximum generation token limit
